findings.evidence: Sibling abbrev ships restatements: code/TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/UniversalCover/Deck/NormalizerQuotientConjugation.lean:54 (@[simp] normalizerQuotientConjEquiv_mk), :86, :174; generic API ships them at code/TauCeti/Algebra/Group/NormalizerQuotientConjugation.lean:128 (@[simp] normalizerQuotientEquivMap_mk). This PR's file contains no lemma declarations at all.
findings.fix: Add @[simp] characteristic lemmas keyed on the new symbols: `mem_basepointChangeSubgroup` (from Subgroup.mem_map), `basepointChangeSubgroupEquiv_apply_coe`/`_symm_apply` (from MulEquiv.coe_subgroupMap_apply / subgroupMap_symm_apply), and `basepointChangeNormalizerQuotientEquiv_mk`/`_symm_mk` (from normalizerQuotientEquivMap_mk / _symm_mk), mirroring Deck.normalizerQuotientConjEquiv.
findings.issue: All three declarations (basepointChangeSubgroup:52, basepointChangeSubgroupEquiv:61, basepointChangeNormalizerQuotientEquiv:70) are `noncomputable abbrev` with no own characteristic/automation lemmas; the docstrings expose the body to substitute for restatements ('without any basepoint-specific restatements'). This over-exposes the body in lieu of API and leaves the new head symbols without a stable, annotated interface.

summary: The three basepoint-change transports are thin `abbrev`s that expose their bodies and ship no own characteristic or @[simp] lemmas, relying on the reducible body to surface generic lemmas — inconsistent with the directly analogous Deck.normalizerQuotientConjEquiv, which is a documented abbrev that ships @[simp] _mk/_symm_mk restatements. Provide the characteristic restatements keyed on the new symbols (and prefer hiding the bodies).
